back_translation: After some time, in the days of wheat harvest, Samson visited his wife with a young goat. He said, 'I will go in to my wife in the chamber.' But her father did not let him enter.
Her father said, 'I really thought that you hated her, so I gave her to your companion. Is not her younger sister better than she? Let her be yours instead.'
Samson said to them, 'This time I shall be blameless toward the Philistines, for I am about to do them harm.'
Samson went and caught three hundred foxes. He took torches, turned tail to tail, and placed a torch between each pair of tails.
He set fire to the torches and sent them into the standing grain of the Philistines, and burned both the stacks and the standing grain, as well as the olive vineyards.

The Philistines said, 'Who did this?' They were told, 'Samson, the son-in-law of the Timnite, because he took his wife and gave her to his companion.' So the Philistines went up and burned her and her father with fire.
Samson said to them, 'If you do this, then I will surely take vengeance on you, and after that I will stop.'
He struck them hip and thigh with a great blow, and he went down and dwelt in the cleft of the rock of Etam.
The Philistines went up and encamped in Judah and spread out at Lehi.
The men of Judah said, 'Why have you come up against us?' They said, 'We have come up to bind Samson, to do to him as he has done to us.'

Three thousand men of Judah went down to the cleft of the rock of Etam and said to Samson, 'Do you not know that the Philistines rule over us? What is this you have done to us?' He said to them, 'As they did to me, so I did to them.'
They said to him, 'We have come down to bind you, to give you into the hand of the Philistines.' Samson said to them, 'Swear to me that you yourselves will not fall upon me.'
They said to him, 'No, but we will bind you securely and give you into their hand. We will not kill you.' So they bound him with two new ropes and brought him up from the rock.

When he came to Lehi, the Philistines shouted to meet him. The Spirit of the LORD rushed upon him, and the ropes that were on his arms became like flax that has burned in fire, and his bonds melted from his hands.
He found a fresh jawbone of a donkey, reached out his hand, took it, and struck down with it a thousand men.
Samson said, 'With the jawbone of a donkey I have piled them in heaps; with the jawbone of a donkey I have struck a thousand men.'
When he finished speaking, he cast the jawbone from his hand, and he called that place Ramath-lehi.

He was very thirsty, and he called to the LORD and said, 'You have given through the hand of Your servant this great deliverance. Now shall I die of thirst and fall into the hand of the uncircumcised?'

God split open the hollow that is in Lehi, and water came out of it. He drank, his spirit returned, and he revived. Therefore he called its name En-hakkore (Spring of the Caller), which is in Lehi to this day.
Samson judged Israel in the days of the Philistines for twenty years.
